tax: Terrae Elementalis Major is placed at species rank beneath Terrae (Lithic order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a 4–6 m stone humanoid of coarse dark rock, convergent-fault formation, and ceiling-shaking stride. It is separated from its sibling Terrae elementalis by its 4–6 m coarse dark body from a convergent fault versus the standard form's 2–3 m mixed stone from a single fault; the two share the genus body-plan but never the single-fault formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: coarse granite, gabbro, and iron-oxide inclusions drawn from a convergent fault where multiple pressure lines meet.
ap: Terrae Elementalis Major stands 4–6 m and 20–50 tonnes. Its silhouette is a massive humanoid with a broad flat head, arm-columns wider than a grey-folk torso, and leg-columns that leave half-metre imprints. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: coarse dark gabbro and iron-oxide inclusions visible in the surface — dark grey-black with rust-red veins — unlike the pale mixed standard form; a stride that registers on a tremor-rod at 100 m, and causes loose ceiling stone to fall at 20 m; and arm-columns that score parallel grooves in soft passage walls as it passes, each groove 30–40 cm wide. It moves with a deep slow stride that vibrates the floor at 100 m and causes ceiling-stone to fall at 20 m, and shows deep amber orbitals, the brightest of the three standard Terrae forms, visible at 80 m. Even after dispersal it can be identified from half-metre plinth-impressions pressed 10 cm into soft stone, parallel wall-grooves from passing arm-columns, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Terrae Elementalis Major persists in major fault convergences and deep pressure zones of the lower tiers where multiple fault lines supply enough pressure to sustain a 20–50 tonne mass. It does not breed; it forms where convergent deep fault pressure along two or more intersecting faults animates coarse rock into a massive humanoid body, and recurs whenever the convergent fault re-charges and the massive body re-forms from the same coarse rock in the same zone. It sustains its mass on convergent deep fault pressure, drawing in coarse rock and fault-breccia incorporated into the massive form. Range is bounded by the convergent fault pressure zone: outside that band the body loses cohesion at the junction edges; the major form fragments into boulder-scale debris rather than rubble. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a passage where two fault lines cross and the junction has begun to produce upward-moving coarse fragments, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Terrae Elementalis Major is roused by any quarrying, blasting, or heavy machinery in its fault convergence zone and otherwise lies a standing coarse-stone mass the height of a loaded cart, easily mistaken for a natural block. It claims a single fault convergence zone and its approach passages and answers intrusion with ceiling strikes first to collapse the passage, cutting off retreat, then advancing on the trapped crew. Toward grey-folk it is extremely hazardous and unpredictable; survey crews report that The Forgemasters consult the Stonesong Heralds before any work near a fault convergence, since a woken major form cannot be outrun. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Terrae Elementalis Major: a body of coarse granite, gabbro, and iron-oxide inclusions drawn from a convergent fault where multiple pressure lines meet; immune to crushing, fire, and ordinary tools; the 20–50 tonne mass makes physical displacement impossible for unaided crews; and a defining weakness — the same convergent resonance that formed it — a matched vibration from two directions simultaneously. Its senses run through the medium itself: tremor sensing at 200 m through the convergent fault; it often knows a crew is coming before the crew has found the passage. It tolerates extreme heat, dust, and darkness but fails at matched convergent vibration disrupting the dual-fault pressure. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the 4–6 m dark coarse body, deep amber orbitals, and ceiling-shake stride, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Terrae Elementalis Major are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it strikes ceilings to collapse passages and trap prey; it engulfs smaller prey in the grip of its arm-columns; it incorporates large boulders to repair damage rapidly; and it can set itself as a solid block across a passage mouth, sealing a crew inside. Each is a direct expression of convergent deep fault pressure sustaining a 20–50 tonne mixed-dark-rock humanoid form, so it cannot, for instance, fit into passages under 3 m wide, move quietly, or persist away from its convergent fault. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Terrae Elementalis Major matters because it is the most dangerous single elemental the Throat's working crews are likely to encounter, and its 100 m tremor detection range makes blasting near convergent faults effectively suicidal without prior Stonesong Herald survey. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an extreme territorial hazard requiring Herald consultation and dual-vibration dispersal equipment before any work near a convergent fault, and its presence reshapes routes through the lower fault-convergence zones, now strictly Herald-surveyed before any lease. The Stonesong Heralds classify the major form as the 'third voice' — the stone at its most authoritative, speaking from where the faults agree.
g: Terrae Elementalis Major (Terrae elementalis major) is the great-form earth elemental: a 4–6 m massive Terrae body born of deep fault convergences, capable of bringing down a passage ceiling with a single strike. Documented from the major fault convergences and deep pressure zones of the lower tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a massive humanoid body of coarse granite, gabbro, and iron-oxide inclusions, held by deep convergent fault pressure — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The major form is not a grown-up standard — it is a distinct body-plan that emerges only from convergent faults, where multiple pressure lines meet. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a collapse of coarse granite and gabbro blocks in a rough giant-humanoid outline and no carcass.
